rule: paper_text = (title + ' ' + snippet).lower(); substring check; overlap >=2 forces hit; otherwise verifier decides skeptically

results.title: VIGIL: A Reflective Runtime for Self-Healing LLM Agents
results.reason: Skeptical judgment: explicitly self-healing LLM agent runtime — same problem, different mechanism. Direct prior art.

results.title: VIGIL: A Reflective Runtime for Self-Healing LLM Agents
results.reason: Same VIGIL paper variant.

results.title: VIGIL: A Reflective Runtime for Self-Healing Agents
results.reason: Same VIGIL paper.

results.title: Benchmarking Large Language Models for Autonomous Run-time Error Repair: Toward Self-Healing Software Systems
results.reason: Self-healing software via LLMs — adjacent prior art.

results.title: TraceCoder: A Trace-Driven Multi-Agent Framework for Automated Debugging of LLM-Generated Code
results.reason: Skeptical: trace-driven debugging, no overlap with stress-triggered local crystallization concept.

results.title: An Intelligent Fault Self-Healing Mechanism for Cloud AI
results.reason: Self-healing cloud AI — adjacent domain.

results.title: LLM-Enhanced Intelligent Fault Diagnosis and Self-Healing
results.reason: LLM-enhanced self-healing.

results.title: Self-Healing Software Systems: Lessons from Nature, Powered by AI
results.reason: Nature-inspired self-healing AI — closest umbrella prior art.

results.title: A Survey of LLM-based Software Repair: Taxonomies, Design Paradigms, and Applications
results.reason: Skeptical: software repair survey, no specific mechanism overlap with my concept; field-wide survey but doesn't claim the specific architecture.

results.title: EvoSkills: Self-Evolving Agent Skills via Co-Evolutionary Verification
results.reason: Self-evolving skills via co-evolutionary verification — different mechanism.

results.title: Riddle solved: Why was Roman concrete so durable? | MIT News
results.reason: Mechanical: 4 matches forces hit. Source-domain article on Roman concrete mechanism.

results.title: Prediction of crack repair percentage in self-healing concrete using machine learning
results.reason: Skeptical: ML predicting properties of physical self-healing concrete is reverse direction (ML applied TO concrete, not concrete-as-analogy for AI). Not prior art for LLM-agent architecture proposal.

results.title: Prediction of Healing Performance of Autogenous Healing Concrete Using Machine Learning
results.reason: Skeptical: ML predicting healing concrete performance — reverse direction, not prior art for LLM-agent self-healing architecture.

results.title: AI-driven prediction framework for nano-bio self-healing concrete
results.reason: Skeptical: AI predicting self-healing concrete properties — reverse direction.

results.title: Researchers revealed the secret of 'self-healing' Roman concrete
results.reason: Mechanical: 2 matches forces hit. Source-domain popular article.

results.title: Prediction of Healing Performance of Autogenous Healing Concrete Using Machine Learning
results.reason: No matches; ML for autogenous healing concrete, reverse direction anyway.

results.title: Roman concrete - Wikipedia
results.reason: Mechanical: 4 matches forces hit. Wikipedia source-domain page.

results.title: Hot mixing: Mechanistic insights into the durability of ancient Roman concrete
results.reason: Mechanical: 3 matches forces hit. Science Advances Roman concrete source paper.

results.title: Predicting mechanical properties of self-healing concrete with Trichoderma Reesei Fungus using machine learning
results.reason: Skeptical: ML predicting concrete properties — reverse direction. Not prior art for the LLM agent architecture.

results.title: LLM-based Agents Suffer from Hallucinations: A Survey of Taxonomy, Methods, and Directions
results.reason: Hallucination survey, unrelated.

results.title: Memory for Autonomous LLM Agents: Mechanisms, Evaluation, and Emerging Frontiers
results.reason: Memory survey, unrelated to self-healing mechanism.

disagreements_with_07_hit_miss.reason: 07 used agent judgment to flag TraceCoder as adjacent debugging; skeptical view: 0 overlap and mechanism (trace-driven multi-agent debugging) is too different from stress-triggered local crystallization to count as prior art.
07 used agent judgment that LLM-based software repair survey shows field is occupied; skeptical view: 0 overlap, surveys of repair systems are not specific prior art for this mechanism.

07 flagged as adjacent (ML+self-healing-concrete); skeptical view: this is ML applied TO physical concrete, not concrete-as-analogy for AI architecture — reverse direction and not prior art for the proposed LLM agent design.
Same reverse-direction reasoning: ML predicting concrete healing performance is not prior art for using concrete as analogy for LLM agent runtime.
Same: AI predicting concrete properties is reverse direction.
Same: ML predicting fungal self-healing concrete properties is reverse direction.

note: All 4 forced (overlap>=2) hits agree between 07 and 12. Disagreements are entirely on overlap=1 or overlap=0 papers where skeptical judgment treats reverse-direction ML-applied-to-concrete papers and generic debugging/repair surveys as non-prior-art for the specific LLM agent architecture proposal.
